pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh (line 12) and export_result.sh (line 12). Standard user 'ga' Documents directory confirmed in env.json and install_qblade.sh
Verified in setup_task.sh (line 13) and export_result.sh (line 13). Standard user 'ga' Documents directory confirmed in env.json and install_qblade.sh

Wind turbine rotor speeds typically range from 15-60 RPM (source: https://theroundup.org/how-fast-does-a-wind-turbine-spin/, https://renewablesystems.org/how-fast-do-wind-turbines-spin/). 30 RPM is at the upper-mid range, consistent with fixed-speed control strategies. QBlade documentation and task description confirm this as the target RPM for fixed speed simulations.

TSR (Tip Speed Ratio) of 7.0 is the standard optimal value for three-bladed horizontal-axis wind turbines (HAWT). Multiple sources confirm TSR 6-8 is optimal range, with 7.0 being the most widely-reported standard value (sources: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tip-speed_ratio, https://users.wpi.edu/~cfurlong/me3320/DProject/Ragheb_OptTipSpeedRatio2014.pdf, field measurements confirm peak Cp at λ_opt ≈ 7.5 for 5-MW reference turbine)
Confirmed in install_qblade.sh (line 71: downloads QBlade_linux_v0.96.2_64bit.zip from SourceForge) and in evidence_docs/README.md (line 4: 'QBlade v0.96.2 64-bit')

pi_items.source: Specified in task.json description (lines 3-6). Matches standard wind turbine control strategies: fixed-speed maintains constant RPM using generator control; variable-speed uses torque control to maximize power coefficient (Cp) at each wind speed

privileged_info_summary: The task uses QBlade v0.96.2, a free open-source wind turbine simulation software. All specified parameters are physically reasonable and standard in wind turbine engineering: fixed-speed control at 30 RPM is within typical rotor speed ranges (15-60 RPM), and variable-speed optimization with TSR 7.0 is the industry-standard design target for three-bladed turbines. Airfoil data comes from the UIUC Airfoil Coordinates Database (real reference data), and sample projects are bundled with QBlade. Output paths are standard user directories (/home/ga/Documents/). The simulation range (3-25 m/s) is standard for turbine power curve analysis.
